original_hypothesis.title: Borrower Can Set fixedTermEndTime to Zero Eliminating Withdrawal Lock for Fixed-Term Markets
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: FixedTermLoanHooks.setFixedTermEndTime only checks that newFixedTermEndTime <= hookedMarket.fixedTermEndTime (cannot increase). Setting newFixedTermEndTime to 0 passes this check for any existing value. Since onQueueWithdrawal checks `if (market.fixedTermEndTime > block.timestamp)` and 0 is never > block.timestamp, setting the term to 0 immediately allows all lenders to queue withdrawals. The borrower could use this after reducing APR to release lenders from a now-unfavorable position, or combine with other tactics.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While the monotonic decrease of fixedTermEndTime is by design, allowing it to go to 0 (effectively disabling the fixed term entirely) may not be the intended behavior. A minimum remaining term or a floor above 0 would prevent this.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Only check is newFixedTermEndTime > hookedMarket.fixedTermEndTime → revert. Setting to 0 always passes since 0 <= any uint32. No minimum floor or non-zero requirement.

preconditions: Market was created with a non-zero fixedTermEndTime

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: No minimum value or non-zero check for newFixedTermEndTime
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Setting to 0 effectively disables the fixed term lock entirely

analysis_questions: Is setting fixedTermEndTime to 0 intentional behavior?
Could borrower exploit this after reducing APR to trigger mass withdrawals?
Does the documentation specify a minimum term guarantee?
validation_requirements: Call setFixedTermEndTime(market, 0) and verify withdrawals are immediately allowed
Test the complete scenario: reduce APR, set term to 0, trigger withdrawals

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Borrower creates market with 365-day term, attracts lenders. After some time, borrower sets fixedTermEndTime to 0. All lenders can now queue withdrawals immediately, potentially causing a bank run. Borrower may have already extracted most borrowed funds.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Fixed-term guarantee is eliminated; lenders lose the fixed-term protection they expected
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All lenders on the fixed-term market
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Cannot be undone (fixedTermEndTime cannot be increased)

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test for setting fixedTermEndTime to 0

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
